filters.term: apply_overrides=0 levels=hml rows=-1 min_qod=70 first=1 sort-reverse=severity notes=1 overrides=1

result.name: Check for discard Service (TCP)

nvt.name: Check for discard Service (TCP)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=The remote host is running a 'discard' service. This service typically sets up a listening socket and will ignore all the data which it receives.
This service is unused these days, so it is advised that you disable it.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=- Under Unix systems, comment out the 'discard' line in /etc/inetd.conf and restart the inetd process
- Under Windows systems, set the following registry key to 0:
HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\SimpTCP\Parameters\EnableTcpDiscard
Then launch cmd.exe and type:
net stop simptcp
net start simptcp
To restart the service.
Notes:
Some firewall devices are known to discard packets on the checked port 9/tcp or mimic such a service via other means. Scanning through a firewall should be generally avoided (Please see references).
If the target was scanned through a firewall and it was determined / has been verified that no such service is running on the target please either:
- create an override for this result
- configure the firewall in a way that port 9/tcp is not reported as being open during the port scanning phase / it is not allowed to open a TCP connection to port 9/tcp on the target|vuldetect=Checks whether a discard service is exposed on the target host by evaluating the info from the following service detection:
- discard Service Detection (TCP) (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.113757)|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
- Under Unix systems, comment out the 'discard' line in /etc/inetd.conf and restart the inetd process
- Under Windows systems, set the following registry key to 0:
HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\SimpTCP\Parameters\EnableTcpDiscard
Then launch cmd.exe and type:
net stop simptcp
net start simptcp
To restart the service.
Notes:
Some firewall devices are known to discard packets on the checked port 9/tcp or mimic such a service via other means. Scanning through a firewall should be generally avoided (Please see references).
If the target was scanned through a firewall and it was determined / has been verified that no such service is running on the target please either:
- create an override for this result
- configure the firewall in a way that port 9/tcp is not reported as being open during the port scanning phase / it is not allowed to open a TCP connection to port 9/tcp on the target

result.description: The discard service was detected on the target host.

nvt.name: Check for Chargen Service (TCP)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:P|summary=The remote host is running a 'chargen' service.|insight=When contacted, chargen responds with some random characters (something like all the characters in the alphabet in a row). When contacted via TCP, it will continue spewing characters until the client closes the connection.
The purpose of this service was to mostly to test the TCP/IP protocol by itself, to make sure that all the packets were arriving at their destination unaltered. It is unused these days, so it is suggested you disable it, as an attacker may use it to set up an attack against this host, or against a third party host using this host as a relay.|affected=|impact=An easy attack is 'ping-pong' in which an attacker spoofs a packet between two machines running chargen. This will cause them to spew characters at each other, slowing the machines down and saturating the network.|solution=- Under Unix systems, comment out the 'chargen' line in /etc/inetd.conf and restart the inetd process
- Under Windows systems, set the following registry keys to 0 :
HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\SimpTCP\Parameters\EnableTcpChargen
HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\SimpTCP\Parameters\EnableUdpChargen
Then launch cmd.exe and type :
net stop simptcp
net start simptcp
To restart the service.|vuldetect=Checks whether a chargen service is exposed on the target host.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
- Under Unix systems, comment out the 'chargen' line in /etc/inetd.conf and restart the inetd process
- Under Windows systems, set the following registry keys to 0 :
HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\SimpTCP\Parameters\EnableTcpChargen
HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\SimpTCP\Parameters\EnableUdpChargen
Then launch cmd.exe and type :
net stop simptcp
net start simptcp
To restart the service.

result.name: Check for Quote of the Day (qotd) Service (TCP)

nvt.name: Check for Quote of the Day (qotd) Service (TCP)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:P|summary=The Quote of the Day (qotd) service is running on this host.|insight=A server listens for TCP connections on TCP port 17. Once a connection is established a short message is sent out the connection (and any data received is thrown away). The service closes the connection after sending the quote.
Remark: NIST don't see 'configuration issues' as software flaws so the referenced CVE has a severity of 0.0. The severity of this VT has been raised by Greenbone to still report a configuration issue on the target.|affected=|impact=An easy attack is 'pingpong' which IP spoofs a packet between two machines running qotd. This will cause them to spew characters at each other, slowing the machines down and saturating the network.|solution=- Under Unix systems, comment out the 'qotd' line in /etc/inetd.conf and restart the inetd process
- Under Windows systems, set the following registry keys to 0 :
HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\SimpTCP\Parameters\EnableTcpQotd
HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\SimpTCP\Parameters\EnableUdpQotd
Then launch cmd.exe and type :
net stop simptcp
net start simptcp
To restart the service.|vuldetect=|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
- Under Unix systems, comment out the 'qotd' line in /etc/inetd.conf and restart the inetd process
- Under Windows systems, set the following registry keys to 0 :
HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\SimpTCP\Parameters\EnableTcpQotd
HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\SimpTCP\Parameters\EnableUdpQotd
Then launch cmd.exe and type :
net stop simptcp
net start simptcp
To restart the service.

nvt.name: echo Service Reporting (TCP + UDP)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=An echo Service is running at this Host via TCP and/or UDP.|insight=The echo service is an Internet protocol defined in RFC 862. It was originally proposed for testing and measurement of round-trip times in IP networks. While still available on most UNIX-like operating systems, testing and measurement is now performed with the Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP), using the applications ping and traceroute.|affected=|impact=|solution=Disable the echo Service.|vuldetect=Checks whether an echo service is exposed on the target host.|solution_type=Mitigation

result.name: DCE/RPC and MSRPC Services Enumeration Reporting

nvt.name: DCE/RPC and MSRPC Services Enumeration Reporting
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=Distributed Computing Environment / Remote Procedure Calls (DCE/RPC) or MSRPC services running on the remote host can be enumerated by connecting on port 135 and doing the appropriate queries.|insight=|affected=|impact=An attacker may use this fact to gain more knowledge about the remote host.|solution=Filter incoming traffic to this ports.|vuldetect=|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution: Filter incoming traffic to this ports.

result.description:
Here is the list of DCE/RPC or MSRPC services running on this host via the TCP protocol:
Port: 2103/tcp
UUID: 1088a980-eae5-11d0-8d9b-00a02453c337, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[2103] Annotation: Message Queuing - QM2QM V1
UUID: 1a9134dd-7b39-45ba-ad88-44d01ca47f28, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[2103] Annotation: Message Queuing - RemoteRead V1
UUID: 76d12b80-3467-11d3-91ff-0090272f9ea3, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[2103] Annotation: Message Queuing - QMRT V2
UUID: fdb3a030-065f-11d1-bb9b-00a024ea5525, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[2103] Annotation: Message Queuing - QMRT V1
Port: 2105/tcp
UUID: 1088a980-eae5-11d0-8d9b-00a02453c337, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[2105] Annotation: Message Queuing - QM2QM V1
UUID: 1a9134dd-7b39-45ba-ad88-44d01ca47f28, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[2105] Annotation: Message Queuing - RemoteRead V1
UUID: 76d12b80-3467-11d3-91ff-0090272f9ea3, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[2105] Annotation: Message Queuing - QMRT V2
UUID: fdb3a030-065f-11d1-bb9b-00a024ea5525, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[2105] Annotation: Message Queuing - QMRT V1
Port: 2107/tcp
UUID: 1088a980-eae5-11d0-8d9b-00a02453c337, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[2107] Annotation: Message Queuing - QM2QM V1
UUID: 1a9134dd-7b39-45ba-ad88-44d01ca47f28, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[2107] Annotation: Message Queuing - RemoteRead V1
UUID: 76d12b80-3467-11d3-91ff-0090272f9ea3, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[2107] Annotation: Message Queuing - QMRT V2
UUID: fdb3a030-065f-11d1-bb9b-00a024ea5525, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[2107] Annotation: Message Queuing - QMRT V1
Port: 49664/tcp
UUID: 12345778-1234-abcd-ef00-0123456789ac, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49664] Named pipe : lsass Win32 service or process : lsass.exe Description : SAM access
UUID: 51a227ae-825b-41f2-b4a9-1ac9557a1018, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49664] Annotation: Ngc Pop Key Service
UUID: 8fb74744-b2ff-4c00-be0d-9ef9a191fe1b, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49664] Annotation: Ngc Pop Key Service
UUID: b25a52bf-e5dd-4f4a-aea6-8ca7272a0e86, version 2 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49664] Annotation: KeyIso
Port: 49665/tcp
UUID: d95afe70-a6d5-4259-822e-2c84da1ddb0d, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49665]
Port: 49666/tcp
UUID: f6beaff7-1e19-4fbb-9f8f-b89e2018337c, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49666] Annotation: Event log TCPIP
Port: 49667/tcp
UUID: 3a9ef155-691d-4449-8d05-09ad57031823, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49667]
UUID: 86d35949-83c9-4044-b424-db363231fd0c, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49667]

UUID: 0b6edbfa-4a24-4fc6-8a23-942b1eca65d1, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49668]
UUID: 12345678-1234-abcd-ef00-0123456789ab, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49668] Named pipe : spoolss Win32 service or process : spoolsv.exe Description : Spooler service
UUID: 4a452661-8290-4b36-8fbe-7f4093a94978, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49668]

UUID: 367abb81-9844-35f1-ad32-98f038001003, version 2 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49669]
Port: 49681/tcp
UUID: 1088a980-eae5-11d0-8d9b-00a02453c337, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49681] Annotation: Message Queuing - QM2QM V1
UUID: 1a9134dd-7b39-45ba-ad88-44d01ca47f28, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49681] Annotation: Message Queuing - RemoteRead V1
UUID: 76d12b80-3467-11d3-91ff-0090272f9ea3, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49681] Annotation: Message Queuing - QMRT V2

Port: 49749/tcp
UUID: 6b5bdd1e-528c-422c-af8c-a4079be4fe48, version 1 Endpoint: ncacn_ip_tcp:192.168.170.135[49749] Annotation: Remote Fw APIs
Note: DCE/RPC or MSRPC services running on this host locally were identified. Reporting this list is not enabled by default due to the possible large size of this list. See the script preferences to enable this reporting.

result.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure

nvt.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.|insight=The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.|affected=|impact=This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.|solution=Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)|vuldetect=Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)

result.description: The following response / ICMP packet has been received: - ICMP Type: 14 - ICMP Code: 0

source.description: Microsoft Message Queuing (MSMQ) RCE Vulnerability (951071) - Active Check
Microsoft Windows SMB Server NTLM Multiple Vulnerabilities (971468)

detail.name: OS
detail.value: Microsoft Windows Server 2022, Microsoft Windows Server 2019, Microsoft Windows Server 2016 or Microsoft Windows 10

detail.name: Services
detail.value: 2105,tcp,msrpc,A DCE/RPC or MSRPC service seems to be running on this port

detail.name: Services
detail.value: 135,tcp,epmap,A DCE endpoint resolution service seems to be running on this port.

source.description: Microsoft IIS Information Disclosure Vulnerability (MS00-058) - Active Check
Microsoft IIS WebHits ISAPI Filter Vulnerability (MS00-06) - Active Check
Microsoft Windows SMB2 Negotiation Protocol RCE Vulnerability
